utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey gabriel i'm doctor scott good to see you today i know you've heard about dax is it okay if i tell dax a little bit about you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so gabriel is a 43 -year-old male today here for back pain evaluation and also has a past medical history of diabetes high blood pressure and high cholesterol so gabriel tell me what's going on with your back

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i was working in the yard and you know bent over to pick something up and i got this pain and you know across the lower part of my back and then it went down my left leg and you know it's been going on for about four days and just does n't seem to be getting any better

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay are you a big gardener or this is something that you just started working in the yard

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i know my wife held a gun to my head make me go out there work in the yard and carry some stuff around it's not my not my first choice but

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure sure

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but that day i i lost the i lost the argument

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah yeah that happens to all of this so when this back pain happened so it was basically you were lifting you were bending down to lift something up and you had the sharp pain going down your right leg you said

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: left leg

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: left leg okay got it sorry and any weakness or numbness in your legs or just the pain mostly

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: in in certain positions i get some tingling but no mostly just pain

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and any loss of bowel or bladder function at all or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and have you had any back surgeries or back problems in the past or this is kind of the first time

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no surgeries you know i've i've had back pain occasionally over the years

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay have you had any any have you tried anything for pain for this have you tried any any medications at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've had ibuprofen it it helped some

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay got it alright well i'll i'll examine you in a second but before we do that let's talk about some of the other conditions that we're kinda following you for i'm looking at your problem list now and you've got a history of diabetes and you're on metformin five hundred milligram twice a day and your how are you doing with your blood sugars and your and your diet and exercise

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i i check my sugar two or three times a week most of the time it's in that one twenty to one forty range

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i take my medicine okay my diet is alright you know i could be fifteen pounds lighter that would be alright but

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i i think the sugar has been okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay we checked your hemoglobin a1c last time i'm looking at your records in epic and it showed that it was you know seven . one so it's it's it's good but it could be better any you know we talked about it controlling your diet or improving your diet and trying to have a balanced meal and not eating some of these sweets and high sugar items how is that going i know you had talked about your wife being a great cook and making cookies and that's hard to stay away from obviously how are things going with that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah she still makes cookies and i still eat them but you know we are trying to trying to do better trying to stay away from more of those carbs and focus on you know less carby less sweet stuff

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright yeah that's always a struggle i certainly understand but you know really important with your diabetes just to prevent some of the complications like kidney failure and eye problems and just keep your sugar under balance so i'll order another hemoglobin a1c today we'll check that again today and and you know just reemphasizing the controlling your diet and exercise is super important and then we'll have those results back we'll we'll see if we need to make any modifications okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your high blood pressure your blood pressure in the clinic looks pretty good it's about one twenty over seventy right now we have you on norvasc five milligrams once a day how are things going with that are you are you checking that periodically or any issues with that at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i guess i check it maybe once a week or two or three times a month and it it the vast majority of the time when i check it it's good usually either that one twenty to one thirty over seventy to eighty range i i think the blood pressure's okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have n't had any real problems there i i have had some some swelling in my ankles though

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay is that new or is that been going on for a while

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well it it started maybe i do n't know a month or two after i started the norvasc

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i was just wondering if the two might be related

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i mean certainly it could be it is you know sometimes that medication can cause that so i'll i'll examine you in a second and see if we need to make any modifications okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so and your anything else bothering you today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i'm we're doing okay i think

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let me examine you for a second i'm gon na go ahead and gabriel i'm gon na do my magic exam now let's pretend i i'm just gon na verbalize some of my findings as i do my exam and so

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: these are like my video visit exams

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: exactly so your neck exam has no jvd there is no bruits that i can hear your lung exam no rales no wheezing on your heart exam you do have a two over six systolic ejection murmur you had that in the past so i'm not too worried about that otherwise regular rate and rhythm on your heart exam on your on your on your belly exam is nice and soft on your back exam you do have some tenderness on the left paraspinal area right where i'm pressing right there your straight leg raise test is negative your reflexes are normal you have some just some tenderness in the lower back in the paraspinal area of your back when i palpate there otherwise your neurological exam is normal on your extremity exam you do have this one plus nonpitting edema of your lower extremities which is a little bit of swelling in your ankles no calf tenderness negative homans sign no signs of blood clot that's what that means so let me just review what you know explain what all this means so the back pain the first problem that you're here today for i think this is more of a muscular sprain i'm gon na recommend we start you on some anti-inflammatory naprosyn five hundred five hundred milligrams twice a day and flexeril ten milligrams twice a day as well i'm gon na refer you to for for physical therapy to help strengthen some of the muscles in your lower back i do n't think you need an x-ray at this stage why do n't we start with physical therapy and the muscle relaxers and the pain medicines if it does n't get better then we can get an x-ray but right now i would start with that if that's okay with you any questions about that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay for the diabetes the the second problem that we talked about today i'm gon na order another hemoglobin a1c continue the metformin five hundred milligrams twice a day why do n't we have you come back in about two weeks and we should have some of the results back and we can discuss if we need to make any modifications for that but right now we will continue the course and we will go from there okay for the high blood pressure you do have this one plus edema in your legs i'm gon na go ahead and order some blood work today i'm gon na go ahead and stop the norvasc and we'll put you on some hydrochlorothiazide ten milligrams once a day and if that does n't get if the swelling does n't go away i'm gon na do some more testing for right now let's get some sort off with some cbc and a bmp i'm gon na check your kidney function i'm gon na get another ekg and also i'm gon na get a chest x-ray and we'll go from there but hopefully this will go away once we stop this medication since it started around that time okay okay and i think that's it anything else we forgot about do you need refills for anything

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i i think i'm okay you gave me a year's worth of refills last time we were together

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay sounds great alright thanks gabriel good seeing you again

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good to see you thanks

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 43-year-old male with a history of diabetes, hypertension, and high cholesterol presenting for back pain evaluation.
The patient reports lower back pain radiating to the left leg for four days following a lifting incident.
The doctor asks if the patient has any weakness or numbness in his legs.
The patient reports tingling in the left leg in certain positions but denies weakness.
The doctor asks if the patient has any loss of bowel or bladder function.
The patient denies loss of bowel or bladder function.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of back surgeries or problems.
The patient denies previous back surgeries but reports occasional back pain over the years.
The doctor asks if the patient has tried any medications for the back pain.
The patient reports using ibuprofen for pain relief.
Diabetes
The patient is taking metformin 500 mg twice daily.
The doctor asks about the patient's blood sugar control, diet, and exercise.
The patient reports blood sugar levels between 120-140 mg/dL.
The patient's most recent hemoglobin A1c was 7.1.
The doctor will order a hemoglobin A1c test.
Hypertension
The patient is taking Norvasc 5 mg once daily.
The doctor asks about blood pressure monitoring and any issues with the medication.
The patient reports blood pressure readings typically between 120-130/70-80 mmHg.
The patient reports ankle swelling.

Physical exam findings include no JVD, no bruits, clear lungs, 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, soft abdomen, left paraspinal tenderness, negative straight leg raise, normal reflexes, and 1+ nonpitting edema of lower extremities.
Back pain
The doctor prescribes Naprosyn 500 mg twice daily and Flexeril 10 mg twice daily.
The doctor refers the patient for physical therapy.
The doctor orders a hemoglobin A1c, CBC, BMP, EKG, and chest x-ray, and instructs the patient to follow up in 2 weeks.
The patient will continue metformin 500 mg twice daily and start hydrochlorothiazide 10 mg once daily while discontinuing Norvasc.
